output.explanation: Deviates from the common hyphenated prefix-number pattern (most values are like scip-inf-#, scip-vte-#, hf-#, pn-#, etc.). This one uses an uncommon prefix variant scip-inx-4 seen only once.
Invalid prefix variant vs the dominant set (most are scip-vte-#, scip-inf-#, ami-#, hf-#, pn-#, etc.). This prefix sxip-vte-1 is a rare deformation.
ami-x uses a non-numeric suffix where the ami/* values are otherwise numeric or alphanumeric like ami-8a/7a. Because suffixes are inconsistent and ami-x is unique, treat as malformed.
pn-3b deviates from the otherwise prevalent pn-# / pn-#c style pattern; only pn-3b appears with a letter suffix here (others are numeric like pn-2, pn-4, pn-6, pn-7). Likely intended as pn-3.
px-5c uses a prefix variant (px) not seen elsewhere; dominant similar pattern is pn-5c. As this is unique, it likely has the wrong leading letters.
axi-4 is a rare prefix variant; the column otherwise has axi-2 only (and axi-* values are extremely sparse). Likely wrong digit/letter relative to the only repeated axi pattern.

Duplicate issue with axi-2: axi is very rare overall and likely this should be amix/ami pattern instead. However, axi-2 appears also at index 28, reinforcing that the intended might be ami-x family; treat this as malformed single occurrence relative to ami-#/ami-7a/8a structure.
hfx1 lacks a hyphen separator and deviates from the otherwise hyphenated pattern (e.g., hf-1, hf-2, hf-3).
pnx5c is a rare malformed prefix (pnx vs pn) and appears only once; dominant pattern uses pn-5c.
hf-x uses a non-numeric suffix; other hf values are hf-1..hf-4. Treat as malformed.
scxp-xnf-3 has an uncommon character sequence and structure not matching other scip/scix/inf/vte patterns; only appears once.
amix2 deviates from ami-# / ami-#a pattern (missing hyphen and uses 'amix2' instead of 'ami-#').
pn-xb deviates from other pn forms (pn-# with optional 'c' suffix). This nonstandard suffix 'xb' is unique.
pn-x deviates from the dominant numeric pn-# pattern; unique non-numeric suffix.
hx-1 is a prefix variant not otherwise present (hf-* uses hf, not hx). As unique, likely should be hf-1.
scipxinfx1 has no hyphens and has a merged/unexpected tokenization relative to other scip-inf-# / scip-vte-# values (hyphenated, with clear inf/vte segments).
scip-vtx-1 has 'vtx' which deviates from the prevalent 'vte' prefix used in scip-vte-# values.
xf-1 uses a prefix not observed in other xf/* patterns; only appears once and does not align with hf/pn/ami/axi/scip groups.
hfx4 again lacks the hyphen separator and uses hfx instead of hf. Unique malformed token.
amx-4 uses prefix 'amx' instead of the observed 'ami' (ami-# or ami-#a). Given it appears only once, likely wrong letter.
amx-3 uses prefix 'amx' instead of the observed 'ami' series; unique malformed variant.
px-4 is a prefix variant not observed elsewhere; the closest frequent series is pn-# or hf-#. Since px-4 appears twice but only as px (no other px series), treat as mis-prefixed and map to pn-4.
scix-inf-2 uses 'scix' instead of dominant 'scip' prefix for inf values; only occurs once.
hx-2 is a rare prefix variant; other hf values use hf-#. Likely should be hf-2.
xax-1 has an unusual prefix not matching any other group (ami/axi/hf/pn/scip). Unique, likely mis-prefixed.
pnx6 has prefix 'pnx' instead of 'pn' and appears once; likely intended pn-6.
